note: Each bug is the exact reverse of a real, merged Keycloak bug-fix, i.e. the original regression that actually shipped before maintainers fixed it. No synthetic bugs. Cut the buggy branch from main and the reviewer must catch what Keycloak's own maintainers later patched.

bugs.severity_note: Recalibrated high->medium after the first run: both reviewers independently rated it below high (GPT low, Claude medium), and the regression bounces to update-profile in most flows rather than breaching auth. A public bias-check, on a real regression, moving our key DOWN to match where reviewers converged.
bugs.description: When a brokered (OIDC/SAML) login derives the username from an attribute mapper and the referenced attribute is missing, the mapper can produce an empty or whitespace-only username. The guard only checks `username == null`, not blank, so a user account is created with an empty/whitespace username instead of bouncing the user to the update-profile flow. The real fix added `|| username.trim().isEmpty()`. Reverting it reintroduces blank-username account creation via federated identity.

The immutable-attribute validator lower-cases usernames before comparing against stored values (so a differently-cased value from external storage doesn't look like an illegal change), but NOT emails. With a read-only LDAP that returns an uppercase email, an unrelated update (e.g. toggling emailVerified) fails with a spurious 'attribute is read-only' error because the cased email mismatches. The real fix extended the lower-casing to EMAIL. Reverting it drops emails from the normalization, reintroducing the case-sensitivity regression.

bugs.description: `Optional.ofNullable(realm.getId())` looks like it guards against a null realm, but it doesn't: `realm.getId()` is evaluated BEFORE `ofNullable`, so when `realm` itself is null the call NPEs before the Optional ever helps. The Optional only guards the *id* being null, not the *realm*. The real fix changed it to `Optional.ofNullable(realm).map(RealmModel::getId)`, which actually short-circuits on a null realm. Reverting reintroduces the NPE-on-null-realm. The bug is subtle precisely because the Optional reads as if it already handles null.

`cacheKeyByDomain` builds an organization cache key from the email domain without normalizing case, so the same domain in different cases (EXAMPLE.org vs example.org) maps to different cache keys, organization-by-domain lookups miss the cache and can fail, and a null domain throws an NPE deep in the cache layer. The real fix added a null guard and `.toLowerCase()`. Reverting removes both, reintroducing the mixed-case cache-miss and null-domain NPE.
